UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents the profile information for a user in the Artesã Aconchegante e-commerce application.

email.description: The user's email address, used for verification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Represents a physical address for shipping or billing within the Artesã Aconchegante application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

state.description: State or province of the address.

postalCode.description: Postal code or ZIP code of the address.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the address was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the address was last updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a customizable rug or crochet kit available in the Artesã Aconchegante product catalog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the product (e.g., 'Tapete de Crochê Clássico').

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

price.description: The base price of the product.

imageUrl.description: URL to the main image of the product.

availableSizes.description: A list of available sizes for this product's customization.

availableColors.description: A list of available colors for this product's customization.

availableMaterials.description: A list of available materials for this product's customization.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was created in the catalog.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product details were last updated.

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a user's current shopping cart in the Artesã Aconchegante application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cart entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was last updated (e.g., item added/removed).

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a single product item, with its customizations and quantity, within a user's shopping cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

quantity.description: The quantity of this specific product item.

selectedSize.description: The size selected for this customized product item.

selectedColor.description: The color selected for this customized product item.

selectedMaterial.description: The material selected for this customized product item.

unitPriceAtAddition.description: The price of the product at the moment it was added to the cart.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the cart item was added.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the cart item was last updated (e.g., quantity change).

OrderItemSummary.title: OrderItemSummary
OrderItemSummary.description: Represents a summary of an item within an order, denormalized for efficient display in lists.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's placed order, including its status and shipping information, in the Artesã Aconchegante application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

orderDate.description: Date and time when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including items, shipping, and taxes.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Processing', 'Shipped', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

shippingAddressId.description: Reference to the Address entity used for shipping this order. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

billingAddressId.description: Reference to the Address entity used for billing this order. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

trackingNumber.description: Tracking number provided by the shipping carrier for this order.

estimatedDeliveryDate.description: Estimated date for the delivery of the order.

items.description: A denormalized summary of items in the order for efficient list display.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order details or status were last updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single product item, with its customizations and quantity, within a placed order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

quantity.description: The quantity of this specific product item within the order.

selectedSize.description: The size selected for this customized product item in the order.

selectedColor.description: The color selected for this customized product item in the order.

selectedMaterial.description: The material selected for this customized product item in the order.

unitPriceAtOrder.description: The price of the product at the moment the order was placed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order item details were last updated.

SecurityLog.title: SecurityLog
SecurityLog.description: Records a security-relevant event in a write-only collection.

userId.description: The ID of the user who triggered the event.

event.description: A description of the security event that occurred.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the log was created.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, unique to each authenticated user. Access is restricted to the owner matching `request.auth.uid`. The `userId` parameter in the path establishes ownership for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores addresses associated with a specific user. This subcollection ensures that only the owning user (`request.auth.uid` matching `userId` in path) can access their addresses, supporting authorization independence. The `userId` field within the document also provides a data integrity check.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this address.

params.name: addressId
params.description: The unique identifier for the address document.

definition.description: Stores the catalog of available products. This is a public collection, allowing all users to read product details for browsing and selection.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product document.

definition.description: Represents a user's current shopping cart. This subcollection ensures that only the owning user (`request.auth.uid` matching `userId` in path) can access their cart.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this cart.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique identifier for the cart document.

definition.description: Stores individual items within a user's shopping cart. Access is restricted to the owning user. The `cartId` field within the document links to the parent cart for data integrity.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the cart containing this item.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent cart document.

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the cart item document.

definition.description: Stores details of orders placed by a specific user. This subcollection ensures that only the owning user (`request.auth.uid` matching `userId` in path) can access their orders, supporting authorization independence. The `userId` field within the document also provides a data integrity check.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who placed this order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order document.

definition.description: Stores individual items within a specific order. Access is restricted to the owning user. The `orderId` field within the document links to the parent order for data integrity.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who placed the order containing this item.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order item document.

definition.description: A write-only collection for security-related logs. Authenticated clients can only create logs; they cannot read, update, or delete them.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for the log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Artesã Aconchegante is meticulously designed for security, scalability, and debuggability, with a strong emphasis on authorization independence and clear access patterns. The chosen strategy minimizes complexity and enforces robust security rules.

Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):
The cornerstone of authorization independence in this design is path-based ownership. All user-specific data, including UserProfile, Address, Cart, and Order, are organized under a `/users/{userId}` root path. This hierarchical nesting allows Firebase Security Rules to directly match `request.auth.uid` against the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This crucial design choice eliminates the necessity for `get()` operations to fetch parent documents for authorization checks. For instance, accessing an address at `/users/{userId}/addresses/{addressId}` only requires a rule to verify `request.auth.uid == userId` from the request path, making the authorization decision atomic and independent of any data retrieval. Furthermore, existing fields like `userId` (in UserProfile, Address, Cart, Order) within documents, or `cartId`/`orderId` (in CartItem, OrderItem), serve to bolster data integrity and provide explicit consistency checks, though their primary role for authorization is superseded by the path variables.

QAPs (Rules are not Filters):
This structure inherently supports Query-As-Permissions (QAPs) through rigorous structural segregation. User-specific collections such as `/users/{userId}/addresses`, `/users/{userId}/carts`, and `/users/{userId}/orders` are logically and physically confined to a single user, as identified by the `{userId}` in their path. Consequently, security rules can precisely grant access solely when `request.auth.uid` aligns with this path's `userId`. This ensures that any `list` operation performed by an authenticated user on these collections will, by design, return only their permissible data, obviating the need for complex filtering within security rules or application logic. Conversely, the `/products` collection is designed for global, public read access, allowing any `list` query to retrieve the entire catalog without authorization-based filtering. This architectural approach guarantees that security rules act as definitive gates for data access, rather than inefficient post-query filters.

Overall, this design strategy results in security rules that are concise, unambiguous, highly robust, and easy to audit, while simultaneously ensuring optimal scalability and performance for the Artesã Aconchegante application.
